data.body:
CONTAINER review (G4 — child consistency), parent c81c-cbcd-17a0-43d2 (plan-review fan-out optimization). Reviewed THIS child (S3 — parallelize container fan-out + warm-then-fan-out + budget-cap fix) WHOLE against the complete sibling roster (S1 0250, S2 c6e5, S4 98c6, S5 1762, S6 da34) and the parent. Checked all 7 cross-child interaction modes. NO G4 FINDINGS.

Affirmations (grounded in src/rebar/llm/plan_review/pass1.py:114-250 and sizing.py:60-71,190-242):
- IMPLICIT SHARED STATE: S3, S4, S5 all touch the sizing.py container-cost projection and the _run_container call structure (pass1.py). This is real shared state, but the parent's Sequencing note + DAG (S3->S4->S5) STRICTLY serialize it ("S4/S5's sizing.py container-cost projections are only correct AFTER S3's 'container never shed' + cost-model fix lands"). S3 is foundational/first; each later landing layers a correct projection on the prior (container_floor_usd: S3 = len(container)*n_children*COST_AGENT at sizing.py:232; S4 -> N; S5 -> <N). No contradiction.
- CONFLICTING ASSUMPTIONS: S3's warm-then-fan-out premise (warm WRITES the prefix, fan-out READS it) is the exact behavior S1 delivers and S1's de-risk E2 empirically confirmed (cold-concurrent x3 => 3 writes/0 reads; warm-first => 0 writes/3 reads). No sibling assumes G3/G4 can be shed; S3's "container NEVER shed" (sizing.py:198-206) is consistent with the parent fidelity AC and with S4/S5's eval-gating, which assume the container always runs. No conflict.
- DEPENDENCY GAP: S1 (0250) blocks ba7e (dep link present); ba7e blocks S4 (98c6) (dep link present). Byte-stability (an S1 AC) is the precondition S3's warm-then-fan-out relies on, correctly upstream. No gap.
- SCOPE OVERLAP: S3 fences out S1/S4/S5 and each fences S3 reciprocally. Shared files pass1.py (_run_container) and sizing.py are split as SERIAL layered edits under the strict S3->S4->S5 ordering (S3 builds the new pool + warm + never-shed/cost-model fix; S5 later adds bin-packing inside the same structure; S4/S5 take the sizing projection N->/<N), not concurrent contradictory ownership. Benign under ANTI-FP.
- ORDERING VIOLATION: none — S3 is correctly the HEAD of the S3->S4->S5 chain and the first to touch the shared projection; the Sequencing note pins this.
- CONSUMER IMPACT: S3's "container criteria are NEVER shed; the cap bounds only overlay/agent spend" + the cost-model correction is consumed (not contradicted) by S4/S5's projections and by the parent budget AC. The shed_to_budget code already reflects S3's guarantee (sizing.py:198-242: container returned unchanged, container_floor recorded for observability). Producer->consumer is DAG-ordered. Consistent.
- RESIDUAL REFERENCES: no sibling carries a stale contract against a design S3 removes. S3's budget-cap change removes the shed-G3/G4 path; no sibling references the centrality-inversion or assumes container shedding. Consistent.

Non-G4 note (intra-child editorial only, below the cross-child/high-confidence threshold — recorded, not a finding): the child's "What" bullet and original ACs still carry the SUPERSEDED framing — "await its first streamed token" (the runner is non-streaming) and "Fix the budget cap that scales with centrality (sizing.py base*(1+centrality))" implying centrality-inversion. The child's own Remediation explicitly reverses BOTH (warm = first real pairing to completion; do NOT invert centrality — keep base*(1.0+ctx.centrality) at sizing.py:71 and instead never-shed container + fix the cost model). These are stale body/AC labels superseded by the same ticket's Remediation, not a cross-child inconsistency; they read benignly under the ANTI-FP filter.
